acceptance_criteria.description: TypeScript compiles with no errors after adding all CLI entry points and report module
CLI entry points are thin wrappers that parse arguments, load config, delegate to library functions, and exit with appropriate codes
acceptance_criteria.rubric: Verify: (1) run-pipeline.ts parses a spec-id argument, loads config from pipeline/config.json (optional), calls runPipeline(), writes a summary report via report.ts, exits 0 on success or 1 on failure/escalation. (2) validate-spec.ts, validate-impl.ts, validate-task.ts each parse a document ID argument, call the appropriate per-level Ring 0 validator, and exit with 0/1 based on pass/fail — suitable for PostToolUse hooks. (3) validate-cross.ts parses a root spec ID, runs all cross-level invariant validators, and reports results. (4) refine.ts (src/cli/refine.ts) parses a document ID and level, calls refine(), reports promoted/escalated. (5) scope-guard.ts accepts a file path and task ID, checks if the file is within the task's scope.files, exits 0 if allowed or 1 if blocked — suitable for PreToolUse hook. (6) report.ts exports a function that generates a summary JSON report to pipeline/reports/ containing run ID, root spec ID, timestamps, overall status, per-document results, and aggregate statistics (documents validated, cross-level checks, LLM calls, token usage, iterations, escalations) per REQ-16. PASS if all six hold; FAIL if any is missing.

acceptance_criteria.description: All CLI entry points follow consistent patterns: argument parsing, config loading, error handling, and exit codes
acceptance_criteria.rubric: Verify: (1) All CLI files use process.argv for argument parsing (no external CLI framework required). (2) All wrap their main logic in a try/catch that prints errors to stderr and exits with code 1. (3) All use consistent naming: kebab-case files, camelCase functions. (4) scope-guard.ts reads the task definition JSON to get scope.files and performs path matching. (5) Files that are thin wrappers (validate-*.ts, refine.ts) contain no business logic — only argument parsing, delegation, and output formatting. PASS if all five hold; FAIL if any is missing.
